title: Melbourne tobacco shop targeted by armed arsonists
fullText: Police are investigating an arson attack on a Glenroy tobacco shop that previously been targeted by arsonists.Police said two male offenders, one armed with a shotgun, entered the store on Belair Avenue just before 10pm last night.Investigators were told the men poured petrol on the floor of the business and lit it before fleeing in a white sedan.Police are investigating whether the arson attack is linked to previous incidents at the shop. (ABC News)Two staff members who were inside the shop at the time managed to put out the fire and were not injured.The white sedan was seen heading south on Pascoe Vale Road and was later found burnt out in Becket Street, Glenroy.Two staff members were in the shop at the time of the incident but were not hurt. (ABC News)No arrests have been made and an arson chemist and detectives will attend the scene later today.Victoria Police said investigations are ongoing and detectives will look into whether the arson is linked to previous incidents at the shop.According to figures from the Gang Crime Squad, there have now been more than 120 tobacco store fires since March, 2023.Anyone with information or footage of the incident has been urged to contact CrimeStoppers on 1800 333 000.
summary: A Glenroy tobacco shop, which has previously been targeted by arsonists, was set alight by armed offenders last night.
